item.title: The unreal they take for real, The real they take for unreal. They never reach the real, By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct. Having…
item.description:
The unreal they take for real,
The real they take for unreal.
They never reach the real,
By their wrong thoughts and wrong conduct.

Having known the real as real,
And the unreal as unreal,
They reach the real,
By their right thoughts and right conduct.

From: Dhammapada 1-20
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: When going to an assembly or a gathering, Or when alone with a single person, Do not speak falsehood, nor cause others to speak it,…
item.description:
When going to an assembly or a gathering,
Or when alone with a single person,
Do not speak falsehood, nor cause others to speak it,
Nor approve of false speech. Abandon all that is untrue.

Do not live a life of drinking intoxicants.
A lay follower who has accepted this Dhamma
Should not cause others to drink nor approve of drinking.
Knowing that drinking leads to madness.

Only fools, when drunk, do evil deeds
And cause other heedless people to live heedlessly.
Avoid this ground of non-merit,
Which deludes and delights the fool.

From: Sutta Nipāta 2.14
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Friends, what is the cessation of suffering? It is the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, its abandonment, relinquishment, liberation, and non-attachment. Friends,…
item.description:
Friends, what is the cessation of suffering? It is the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, its abandonment, relinquishment, liberation, and non-attachment. Friends, what is the path leading to the cessation of suffering? It is just this Noble Eightfold Path leading to the cessation of suffering, namely: Right View, Right Intention, Right Speech, Right Action, Right Livelihood, Right Effort, Right Mindfulness, Right Concentration.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 9 – Sammādiṭṭhisutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Brahmin, for what reason, for what condition, given that Rājagaha exists, given that the path to Rājagaha exists, given that you exist as a guide,…
item.description:
Brahmin, for what reason, for what condition, given that Rājagaha exists, given that the path to Rājagaha exists, given that you exist as a guide, though you instruct and teach thus, one person takes the wrong path and goes westward, while another person can reach Rājagaha safely?" — "Master Gotama, what can I do? I am only a guide, Master Gotama." — "So too, brahmin, Nibbāna exists, the path to Nibbāna exists, and I exist as a guide. But my disciples, instructed and taught thus by me, some attain the ultimate goal of Nibbāna, some do not attain it. Here, brahmin, what can I do? The Tathāgata is only a guide.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 107 – Gaṇakamoggallānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: 'The cutting off of the conduit of rebirth, the cutting off of the conduit of rebirth,' Venerable Sir, is said. What is the conduit of…
item.description:
'The cutting off of the conduit of rebirth, the cutting off of the conduit of rebirth,' Venerable Sir, is said. What is the conduit of rebirth, Venerable Sir? What is the cutting off of the conduit of rebirth?" - "Rādha, any desire, lust, delight, craving, any engagement, mental adherence, underlying tendency regarding form — that is called the conduit of rebirth. Their cutting off is the cutting off of the conduit of rebirth. ... regarding feeling ... regarding perception ... regarding formations ... Any desire, lust, delight, craving, any engagement, mental adherence, underlying tendency regarding consciousness — that is called the conduit of rebirth. Their cutting off is the cutting off of the conduit of rebirth.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 23.3
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, bhikkhus, a person reflects thus: 'For one who does evil bodily action, there is an evil result in the hereafter; for one who does…
item.description:
Here, bhikkhus, a person reflects thus: 'For one who does evil bodily action, there is an evil result in the hereafter; for one who does evil verbal action... evil mental action, there is an evil result in the hereafter. What if after death, with the breakup of the body, I were to be reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell?' That person, through fear of an evil destination, abandons evil bodily action and cultivates good bodily action; abandons evil verbal action and cultivates good verbal action; abandons evil mental action and cultivates good mental action; and dwells with a pure self. This, bhikkhus, is called fear of an evil destination.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.121
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, what is the near shore and what is the far shore? Wrong view, bhikkhus, is the near shore; right view is the far shore...…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, what is the near shore and what is the far shore? Wrong view, bhikkhus, is the near shore; right view is the far shore... Wrong liberation is the near shore; right liberation is the far shore. This, bhikkhus, is the near shore; this is the far shore.

Few among humans
Reach the far shore,
While the rest of people
Run along this shore.

Those who practice the Dhamma
According to the well-taught teaching,
Will reach the far shore,
Crossing the realm of death so hard to cross.

The wise, abandoning dark states,
Develop bright states,
Leaving home for homelessness,
In seclusion, hard to delight in.

Seek delight in Nibbāna,
Abandon sensual pleasures, have no obstructions,
The wise cleanse themselves
Of defilements from within.

Those with right mind,
Well develop the factors of enlightenment,
Abandon all attachments,
Rejoicing, not clinging,
Without taints, radiant,
They live at peace in the world.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 10.118
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Venerable Gotama, to what extent is one a brahmin, and what are the qualities that constitute a brahmin? Then the Blessed One, having understood…
item.description:
- Venerable Gotama, to what extent is one a brahmin, and what are the qualities that constitute a brahmin?
Then the Blessed One, having understood the meaning of this, on that occasion uttered this inspired utterance:

That brahmin who has
Abandoned all evil states,
Not proud, without defilements,
Self well-tamed,
Who has mastered the Veda,
And fulfilled the holy life,

That brahmin can indeed utter
The Brahma-word in accordance with Dhamma.
He has no conceit whatsoever
Anywhere in this world.

From: Udāna 1.4
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Sitting to one side, the brahmin Mātaposaka said to the Blessed One: - Venerable Gotama, I seek material food in the proper way. Having sought…
item.description:
Sitting to one side, the brahmin Mātaposaka said to the Blessed One:
- Venerable Gotama, I seek material food in the proper way. Having sought material food in the proper way, I support my mother and father. Venerable Gotama, doing this, am I doing my duty?
- Brahmin, doing this, you are doing your duty. Brahmin, one who seeks material food in the proper way, and having done so, supports his mother and father, that person accumulates much merit.

Whoever in the proper way
Supports mother and father,
Because of this good deed
Toward father and mother,
The wise in this world
Praise that person,
After death, they are reborn
In heaven's bliss.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 7.19
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, the Blessed One Vipassī, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, thought: 'This Dhamma I have realized is deep, hard to see, hard to realize,…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, the Blessed One Vipassī, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, thought: 'This Dhamma I have realized is deep, hard to see, hard to realize, peaceful, sublime, beyond reasoning, subtle, to be understood by the wise. But this generation delights in sensual pleasures, is excited by sensual pleasures, enjoys sensual pleasures. For a generation that delights in sensual pleasures, is excited by sensual pleasures, enjoys sensual pleasures, it is hard to see this principle of conditionality, this dependent origination. It is also hard to see the stilling of all formations, the abandoning of all basis for existence, the destruction of craving, dispassion, cessation, Nibbāna. If I were to teach the Dhamma and others did not understand me, that would be wearisome and troublesome for me.' Bhikkhus, then these wonderful verses, unheard before, arose to the Blessed One Vipassī, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One: 'Why should I now declare what I have realized with such difficulty? This Dhamma is not easily understood by those overcome by greed and hatred. Those stained by craving and shrouded by ignorance find it hard to see this Dhamma. It goes against the stream, subtle, deep, hard to see, and fine.'

From: Dīgha Nikāya 14 – Mahāpadānasutta
📖 Read this sutta
